title: Camshaft Bellows Ventilator — Cam-Follower Respiratory Drive
design_question: How to mechanically drive a bellows for consistent tidal volume delivery in a DIY ventilator?
selected_approach: Eccentric disc cam (50 mm eccentricity) on 12 V DC gear motor shaft (12–20 RPM = BPM). Cam follower rod compresses a self-reinflating silicone bellows (500 mL volume). Follower guided in linear bearing. Spring (15 N/mm) ensures bellows re-expansion on return stroke. Arduino controls motor RPM via PWM (I/E ratio 1:2 by asymmetric cam profile).
rationale: Cam-driven bellows provides positive-pressure ventilation with fixed tidal volume set by cam eccentricity. Simple, reliable, few moving parts. 12 V DC operation portable. Silicone bellows self-seals. Tidal volume = π·d²/4 × stroke = 50 cm² × 10 mm = 500 mL.

alternatives.reason_rejected: BVM more complex to seal automatically.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Cam and Follower Mechanism — Displacement Profiles' confirms max acceleration formula and pressure angle limit.
supporting_fact_titles: Cam and Follower Mechanism — Displacement Profiles
DC Motor Speed Control via PWM
